segments.s470-g17: به is the preposition 'to/for' marking the indirect object; درویش is the recipient (a dervish/ascetic). بخشیم is 1st person plural present/future (بـ- prefix + گفت-stem pattern) meaning 'we give/bestow'; the verb ends in -یم marking 'we'. بسیار precedes the noun for metrical/poetic order and means 'many' modifying چیز 'thing(s)'. Word order here is Preposition + Indirect Object + Verb + Quantifier + Noun, a slightly inverted poetic order.
segments.s470-g18: ز is the poetic variant of از ('from/of') and introduces the source 'دارنده' (the possessor/owner). چیزی in a negative clause functions as 'anything' rather than 'something'. نخواهیم is the negated 1st person plural present/future ('we do not/will not want/ask'); the negative prefix n- is attached to the verb. نیز ('also/too') adds the sense 'as well' or 'either' to the clause. The literal order is 'from the owner anything we will-not (ask) also.'
segments.s470-g19: چو here is the subordinating conjunction 'when'. اسکندر is the subject 'Alexander'. این نیکویها is a demonstrative + plural noun ('these good/virtuous things' or 'these virtues'); the plural ending and demonstrative precede the noun in the usual Persian order. بگفت is a past-tense form 'he said' (ب‑ prefix + گفت), common in classical/persian poetry.
segments.s470-g20: دل پادشا is a genitive/possessive phrase 'the king's heart' with the possessor following the possessed (poetic noun-noun construction for 'دلِ پادشا'). گشت is the past of 'to become/turn' meaning 'became'. با is the preposition 'with'; داد is a noun here meaning 'justice' (not the verb 'gave'); جفت is a predicative noun/adjective 'paired/matched'. The overall structure is Subject (possession phrase) + Verb 'became' + Complement 'paired with justice.'
segments.s470-g21: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'. ایوان 'portico/porch' is the place from which the action issues. برآمد is the past 'rose/issued forth'. یکی here functions as an indefinite pronoun 'someone' (rather than numeral). آفرین is an interjection 'bravo' or 'acclamation' — the words of the person who rose. The phrase order places the source and action before the speaker and his cry.
segments.s470-g22: بران is a contracted poetic locution meaning 'بر آن' or 'to/for that', introducing the object of the acclamation. دادگر is an epithet 'just' (literally 'justice-doer') modifying شهریار 'sovereign/king'. زمین 'earth' follows as a genitive/territorial epithet ('king of the earth'). The chain is an epithet sequence 'بران [آن] دادگر شهریارِ زمین' — 'to that just king, sovereign of the earth.'
segments.s470-g23: ازان پس is a temporal phrase 'after that' (از + آن + پس). پراگنده شد is the compound verb meaning 'was scattered/ dispersed' (پراگنده 'scattered' + شد 'became/was'); here شد is the past auxiliary. انجمن is the subject 'assembly/meeting' that is dispersed. The order follows 'after that then dispersed was the assembly.'
segments.s470-g24: جهاندار 'world-holder/sovereign' is the subject (an honorific for the ruler). بنشست is the past 'sat down' (he took his seat). با introduces the company or manner: رای‌زن (compound noun) 'those who give counsel' — 'counselors'. The literal order is 'the sovereign sat with (his) counselors.'
segments.s470-g9: This is a conditional time clause built with two alternative condition-markers: اگر (if) begins the first possibility and ار (an archaic 'or if') introduces the alternative. گاه ... بار is a poetic collocation meaning 'at times; on some occasions' (گاه as an adverb 'sometimes' + بار 'occasion'), and آید is the present/habitual form 'comes/occurs.' نیم‌شب is an adverbial noun 'at midnight.'
segments.s470-g10: به پاسخ رسد is an impersonal/result clause: به + پاسخ ('to/for the answer') with رسد ('arrives, comes') giving 'a reply comes/arrives.' چون here functions as a temporal conjunction 'when/as,' and گشاید is an archaic/present-subjunctive form of 'to open' meaning 'open(s).' دو لب ('the two lips') is the nominal phrase that precedes the verb, following classical Persian SOV tendencies.
segments.s470-g11: چو is a subordinating conjunction meaning 'when.' پیروزگر is a noun/adjective 'victor' or 'triumphant one,' modified by فرهی ('noble, high-born/cultured'), and دادمان is the verb 'gave/bestowed (to us)' with the enclitic object '-مان' ('us'). The clause thus gives the temporal setting: 'when the noble victor bestowed (something) on us.'
segments.s470-g12: در بخت پیروز is a prepositional phrase 'in / into a victorious fortune' (در = 'in', بخت = 'fate/fortune', پیروز = 'victorious'). بگشادمان is an older/poetic verbal form meaning 'opened for us' (ب‑ prefix plus base + object suffix '-مان'), here describing the effect of the bestowal.
segments.s470-g13: همه ('all') modifies زیردستان ('the subjects/subordinates'). بیابند is the 3rd-person plural present/subjunctive/optative form of یافتن ('to find'/'to be found') used here optatively—'may they be found' or 'let them be found.' بهر is a short prepositional/poetic particle meaning roughly 'for/throughout' (introducing the spatial sweep).
segments.s470-g14: This is a simple list of locations introduced by به ('in/on/at'): به کوه و بیابان و دریا و شهر — 'on mountain and plain/steppe, in sea and in city.' Each coordinate is linked by و ('and'). Together they specify where the subjects may be found.
segments.s470-g15: نخواهیم is the 1st-person-plural present negative of خواستن ('we do not want / we will not ask'). باژ is a poetic noun meaning 'cry/clamor' or 'noise'; از جهان ('from the world') gives the source, and پنج سال ('five years') expresses the temporal span — 'we will not want any clamor from the world for five years.'
segments.s470-g16: جز آنکس ('except that person') introduces the exception. The first که is the relative/complementizer 'who/that,' and گوید is the present 'he says' (introducing reported speech). The second که begins the quoted content 'that I am ...' — هستم is the 1st-person present 'I am,' and همال here is used in the sense 'peer/equal' (an archaic/poetic usage), so the whole phrase reports someone saying 'I am a peer.'
segments.s470-g1: This is a temporal subordinate clause introduced by چو meaning "when"; word order places the subject سکندر first, then the temporal phrase بر تخت ("upon the throne") and the past tense verb بنشست ("sat down"). گفت is the main-reporting verb "he said" introducing what follows.
segments.s470-g2: که is the complementizer introducing reported speech; با + جان شاهان = "with the lives of the kings" (جان = life/soul, شاهان = kings). خرد is the noun "wisdom." باد is the optative particle "may" (poetic), and جفت is the predicative noun "pair" — together forming the wish "may wisdom be paired with the lives of kings."
segments.s470-g3: که here introduces an assertion/explanation: پیروزگر is the subject "the victor/conqueror," در جهان is a locative phrase "in the world," and ایزدست is a poetic fusion meaning "is God" (ایزد + است). The clause states the universal truth that God is the conqueror of the world.
segments.s470-g4: جهاندار ("lord/sovereign") begins a closely linked relative clause. کز is the relative/prepositional element here meaning "from whom," وی = "him." نترسد is the negative present form "does not fear/does not shrink," and بدست (poetic) functions adverbially "by hand/against (his hand)" — together: "the sovereign from whom no one shrinks (in the face of his hand/power)."
segments.s470-g5: بد و نیک = "bad and good," with هم here functioning adverbially as "alike/also"; بگذرد is the verb "pass" (3rd sing.), and بی‌گمان is an adverb "undoubtedly." The clause states a general truth: both good and bad pass.
segments.s470-g6: رهایی is the noun "freedom/escape" and نباشد here functions as a negative present/subjunctive existential sense "there is not" (classical/poetic usage). ز is the poetic preposition "from," چنگ is a metaphorical "claw/grip," and زمان = "time." The phrase means "there is no escape from the claw/grip of time."
segments.s470-g7: هرانکس 'whoever' begins a relative clause introduced by که; آید is the verb "comes." بدین is the prepositional phrase "to this" (here written as ب + دین in the data), and بارگاه is "the royal court/house." Together: "Whoever comes to this court..."
segments.s470-g8: که باشد is a jussive/subjunctive turn meaning "let it be/let him be" or "that he be." ز ما ("from us") and سوی ما (here best read as "in our presence / toward us") indicate the relation to the king: the petitioner approaches the ruler. دادخواه is the noun "seeker of justice/petitioner." The clause amounts to a formal instruction: let anyone who comes be a petitioner of justice before us.

sentences.s470-s9: We bestow many things on the dervish, and we will not ask anything of the owner either.
sentences.s470-s10: When Alexander spoke these good deeds, the king's heart became matched with justice.
sentences.s470-s11: From the portico someone rose, exclaiming 'Bravo to that just sovereign of the earth.'
sentences.s470-s12: After that the assembly was dispersed and the world-ruler sat down with his councilors.
sentences.s470-s5: If at times such an occasion comes, or if it comes at midnight, a reply will arrive when the two lips open.
sentences.s470-s6: When the noble victor bestowed on us, he opened for us a victorious fortune.
sentences.s470-s7: May all the subjects be found everywhere — on mountain and plain, in sea and city.
sentences.s470-s8: We will not desire any clamor from the world for five years, except that one who says, 'I am a peer.'
sentences.s470-s1: When Alexander sat upon the throne, he said, "May wisdom be paired with the lives of kings."
sentences.s470-s2: For the victor in the world is God; the sovereign from whom no one shrinks in fear.
sentences.s470-s3: Good and bad alike pass; undoubtedly there is no escape from the grip of time.
sentences.s470-s4: Whoever comes to this court, let him be (or be regarded) a petitioner of justice toward us/in our presence.
